title: Ayanami Rei in the 5th Dimension Bookend ReisSplit
display_title: Ayanami Rei in the 5th Dimension Bookend ReisSplit
wikitext:
[]: Ayanami Rei in the 5th Dimension (Bookend Rei's)[Split]             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:16 am [Split from "Episode 19 / Toji's "dream"" . - OMF] EoE goes out of its way to establish Rei's fifth dimensional nature. There is no evidence anywhere in the series that she's psychic prior to that or that Rei II has any secret abilities. Rei III manifests an AT field. It's absurdly strong, but everyone does that. Pre-Lilith Rei's supposed telepathy also infers a kind of omniscience, otherwise she'd have no reason to observe someone she'd never heard of or met, one time, for no readily apparent reason. Further, the identical Figure of Rei image appears at the end of EoE after Rei in all her forms, supposing she continues to be bound by fourth dimensional time, is/are dead. Quantum Rei is supported by the appearances of psychopomp Rei before Rei III rejoins Lilith, the quantum gibberish in her birthplace, and her reappearance after her apparent death. Psychic Rei is an explanation to a problem that doesn't exist, full of holes that have to be patched with leaps of logic and assumptions that a fictional character did things once for no reason and it had no impact on anything. Quantum Rei, once you have the correct frame of reference, quickly and easily explains everything without any plot holes or inconsistencies.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:36 am Chuckman wrote: EoE goes out of its way to establish Rei's fifth dimensional nature. There is no evidence anywhere in the series that she's psychic prior to that or that Rei II has any secret abilities. Rei III manifests an AT field. It's absurdly strong, but everyone does that.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:47 am Bagheera wrote: Yes. I've maintained for quite some time that Lilith is connected to all of her creations, and this is obviously the means.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:59 am Chuckman wrote: Yes, because she's a fifth dimensional entity that exists everywhere at once.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 10, 2014 1:39 pm Uh...doesn't the fact that Rei's apparitions show up at multiple places at once, sometimes while Rei is still walking and being conscious in her body, imply quantum shenanigans? Like, being in two places at once is LITERALLY the hallmark of quantum shenanigans. Even if you ignore time travel Rei's soul has special properties no other entity in the series, including Adam/Kaworu, EVER portrays. ...And it's kind of weird Kaworu wouldn't be implied to have similar capabilities; Rei sure as hell copies a lot of his powers, such as the floating. And Rei DID collect souls that died before Instrumentality began. She appears to Misato and Ritsuko when they die and then they appear in Instrumentality and there's shots somewhere of them being tanged at some point IIRC. I honestly see no reason not to think Rei's transcending time in some way except personal bias against the idea. I get what you mean, but the problem is that it doesn't explain everything. It doesn't explain why she'd show up at the start of the show, it doesn't explain why she'd appear before Misato and Ritsuko without collecting their souls, it doesn't explain the business with Touji . . . it doesn't explain anything, really. It's a rationale for the how of things but it pays no attention whatsoever to the why. And the why is a pretty big deal.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 10, 2014 3:44 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Uh...doesn't the fact that Rei's apparitions show up at multiple places at once, sometimes while Rei is still walking and being conscious in her body, imply quantum shenanigans? Like, being in two places at once is LITERALLY the hallmark of quantum shenanigans. Even if you ignore time travel Rei's soul has special properties no other entity in the series, including Adam/Kaworu, EVER portrays.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 10, 2014 4:08 pm FWIW, the Vol. 1 Platinum/Renewal booklet describes bookend Rei as "the existence that gazes upon man", but so far nobody has figured out where that quote is from. The booklets have decent information within, so I wouldn't write them off. (e.g: DC 22' Asuka "wanting love and help from Shinji", and TV 26: "Just like multiple endings in a video game, it's up to the audience to decide if [the TV end] is a good ending or bad ending.")

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 10, 2014 4:19 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: FWIW, the Vol. 1 Platinum/Renewal booklet describes bookend Rei as "the existence that gazes upon man", but so far nobody has figured out where that quote is from.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Jul 10, 2014 4:25 pm Bagheera wrote: why shouldn't Lilith be able to sense her creations in like fashion? That makes for some great symmetry.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 10, 2014 4:37 pm Bagheera wrote: Y Because the SoLs were designed for a purpose, and there's no reason to think transcending space and time entered into that purpose in any way. It's possible the power of the S2 is just so staggering that it can be applied in that fashion (Leliel was certainly flirting with the idea, at least, and the whole Chamber of Guf business does likewise), but there's still no need for it. Mind you, I understand the appeal it has -- despite my arguments against it it holds a lot of appeal for me, too. But the different apparitions and the telepathy are wrinkles that it just doesn't address very well. By contrast, Rei as telepathic sleeping deity addresses pretty much all of it IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 10, 2014 4:44 pm pwhodges wrote: ...wherever they are in time or space - hence the idea of the four-dimensional aspect of Lilith/Rei (which some people are quite unnecessarily calling 5-D).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 10, 2014 5:24 pm the 5d thing is because 4d just implies movement through time, but still existing at a specific point on the timeline. Going 5d allows simultaneous existence across the entire timeline. 6d would allow her to visit alternate timelines.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Jul 10, 2014 5:25 pm Your dimensions are clearly different from mine; but whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 10, 2014 6:19 pm The difference might be if the numbering you're using has a zeroeth dimension or not.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jul 10, 2014 7:58 pm The major problem with the time-travel interpretation of the "bookend Rei's" is that it requires a very awkward extension of the quantum strangeness concepts within the show to include time-travel. While some theories of QM do permit time-travel, there's no direct evidence for this concept in the show. It's something we've taken from external (6th tier?) QM sources and then applied to the various strange occurrences of Rei. In my view, the application of this time-travelling instrumentality Rei theory to Toji's hallucination is the single biggest error this theory introduces. Rei is talking to Shinji, not Lilith/Instrumentality-Rei from the furture/beyond-time. There is enough evidence in the show to support this, as well as supplemental evidence now available from Rebuild. Moreover this theory fails to explain Rei's earlier conversation with Asuka, or why Toji is seeing the Hell-train at all. As to the bookend Rei's themselves, I've argued previously that these could be Avatar's of Lilith -- the one hanging in Terminal Dogma. In short there are alternative ways of explaining the various appearances of Rei, in the hallucination and elsewhere, without needed to resort to adding on time-travel to the Quantum Mechanics canon in the show. Bagheera wrote: Edit: Also, nice argument OMF. We don't often see eye to eye on things but I think you're on target here.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 10, 2014 8:15 pm For whatever it's worth: in PSP NGE2, Shinji's Scenarios have a few hell train visits, but there's no mention of them in Rei 2's scenario (where she mentally talks with both Lilith in Terminal Dogma and Rei 1 in Eva-00).

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 10, 2014 8:27 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: The major problem with the time-travel interpretation of the "bookend Rei's" is that it requires a very awkward extension of the quantum strangeness concepts within the show to include time-travel.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Thu Jul 10, 2014 8:46 pm Chuckman wrote: The alternatives are clunky, inelegant and add nothing to the themes and artistic meaning of the show. When you over-analyze and try to force a work of fiction into a 'realistic' framework eventually you reduce it to a series of things that happen. That is the murder of art.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 10, 2014 9:16 pm How else are to view except in the context of later events in the series?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 10, 2014 9:43 pm It might be a bit ponderous, but the UbeRei observer is the only explanation for the bookeneds that makes any sense to me, and tying it in to the weirdness of 19 seems more plausible to me than anything else thus offered. All bets are off for Rebuild though. Moreover this theory fails to explain Rei's earlier conversation with Asuka, or why Toji is seeing the Hell-train at all.
